title: Satureja baborensis
taxonomy: Satureja baborensis belongs to the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is due to the plant's characteristics, such as its square stem and opposite leaves, which are similar to those of other Lamiaceae species. The genus Satureja is a group of perennial herbs, and Satureja baborensis is a specific species within this genus. The Lamiaceae family is known for its diverse range of plant species, many of which are used for culinary and medicinal purposes. Satureja baborensis is native to North Africa, and its classification reflects its unique combination of characteristics and geographical origins.
